name: Sarria - Becerreá Return
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Sarria, located in Galicia, Spain, traverse a diverse landscape characterized by rolling green hills, lush countryside, and extensive forests. The region offers varied terrain, from sections with gentle gradients suitable for different fitness levels to routes featuring significant elevation gains. Many routes utilize mostly quiet roads, providing a serene cycling experience through charming villages and natural settings. Best road cycling routes around Sarria The most popular road cycling route is Sarria - O Cebreiro Loop, a 65.4 miles (105.3 km) trail that takes 5 hours 38 minutes to complete. This difficult route features over 2,000 meters of elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sarria - Becerreá Return, a difficult 51.3 miles (82.5 km) path. This route winds through the region's characteristic rolling hills and quiet rural roads. Local road cyclists also love the Alto do Poio – Cebreiro Pass loop from Sarria, a 57.2 miles (92.0 km) trail leading through significant mountain passes, often completed in about 5 hours 3 minutes. Road cycling around Sarria is defined by rolling green hills, lush countryside, and quiet roads. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Sarria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Sarria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of road cycling routes are available in Sarria?
A: Sarria offers a diverse range of road cycling routes, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find options from easy and moderate rides, perfect for a relaxed day out, to challenging routes with significant elevation gains for experienced cyclists. The region is characterized by rolling green hills, lush countryside, and quiet rural roads.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Sarria?
A: Yes, Sarria has routes with gentle gradients suitable for beginners and families. While many routes involve some elevation, there are easier options that allow you to enjoy the scenic Galician countryside without excessive difficulty. For example, routes that stick closer to the River Sarria tend to be less demanding.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes around Sarria?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Sarria offers routes with substantial climbs. A notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942804102/sarria-o-cebreiro-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sarria - O Cebreiro Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 65.4 miles (105.3 km) and features over 2,000 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943521635/sarria-becerrea-return" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sarria - Becerreá Return&lt;/a&gt;, a 51.3-mile (82.5 km) path winding through characteristic rolling hills.

Q: What kind of terrain will I encounter on road cycling routes in Sarria?
A: The terrain around Sarria is predominantly characterized by rolling green hills, lush countryside, and extensive forests. Routes often follow well-paved, mostly quiet roads through charming villages. While some sections have gentle gradients, others, particularly those heading towards areas like O Cebreiro, feature significant ascents and descents.

Q: How long do road cycling routes near Sarria typically take to complete?
A: The duration of road cycling routes in Sarria varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. Shorter, moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portomarín Bridge – Horreo of Galicia loop from Ferreiros&lt;/a&gt; (42.1 km) might take around 2 hours 15 minutes. Longer, difficult routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942804102/sarria-o-cebreiro-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sarria - O Cebreiro Loop&lt;/a&gt; (105.3 km), can take over 5 hours 30 minutes.

Q: What interesting sights can I see along the road cycling routes in Sarria?
A: Many routes in Sarria pass by historical and natural attractions. You might encounter traditional Galician &lt;em&gt;hórreos&lt;/em&gt; (stone granaries), ancient churches like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/137061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of St John of Portomarín&lt;/a&gt;, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/716040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Saint Julian of Samos&lt;/a&gt;. Natural features include the serene River Sarria and ancient trees such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/424987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Chestnut Tree in Ramil&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available from Sarria?
A: Yes, several circular road cycling routes start and end in Sarria, allowing you to return to your starting point without retracing your steps. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942804102/sarria-o-cebreiro-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sarria - O Cebreiro Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31813528" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto do Poio – Cebreiro Pass loop from Sarria&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through significant mountain passes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Sarria?
A: The road cycling experience in Sarria is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Cyclists often praise the region's picturesque rolling green hills, the tranquility of the rural roads, and the charming villages encountered along the way. The well-maintained paths and diverse terrain also contribute to a satisfying ride.

Q: Is Sarria a good base for exploring the Camino de Santiago by road bike?
A: Sarria is an excellent base for exploring the Camino de Santiago by road bike, as it's the most popular starting point for the final 100 kilometers of the Camino Francés. This means the routes are well-established, and the region offers ample infrastructure, including accommodation, food, and bike rental services, catering to pilgrims and cyclists alike.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the road cycling routes?
A: Given Sarria's role as a Camino hub, many routes pass through villages and towns that offer various amenities. You'll find places for food and refreshments, especially in larger towns like Portomarín or along the main Camino de Santiago path, ensuring you can refuel during your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Sarria?
A: The best time for road cycling in Sarria is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) months. During these periods, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, with less extreme heat than summer and fewer rainy days than winter, making for ideal cycling conditions through the lush Galician landscape.
tips: Here, in the heart of the Old Town, you will find the municipal hostel of Sarria. If you don't arrive in time to get a place, nothing happens: you will see that there are many more hostels with good prices in the surrounding streets. Of course, to ensure, in the private ones it is convenient to reserve.
Cyclotourist port. Start of the port from Becerrea with broken road. It is better to go up this slope while the road is not fixed and thus avoid going down the bad road.

Quiet secondary road and good asphalt. Through towns. Last section in continuous ascent.
You can extend the ascent by crossing N-VI and going up to the Becerrea Mountain School, achieving about 10 km of port.
